app.title: 4DA
app.description: 4DA reads the internet for developers — privately, locally — and gets sharper every day.
app.descriptionLong: It learns from how you engage with what it shows you. Yesterday's noise becomes tomorrow's signal.
cmdk.emptyHint: Try a topic, a view, or an action
analysis.apiError: API error - check your API key in Settings
analysis.keywordOnly: Scoring via keyword matching with context synthesis. Install Ollama or add an API key in Settings for semantic search.
analysis.briefingAutoGenerated: New briefing auto-generated from critical signals
analysis.stackDetected: Auto-detected your stack: {{stack}}. Scoring will prioritize matching sources.
analysis.calibrationDrift: Calibration curve expired for {{task}} — recalibrating from your next interactions.
status.keywordOnlyTooltip: Keyword matching with context synthesis active. Add an AI provider in Settings for semantic search.
status.degradedTooltip: Embeddings using fallback. Results may be less accurate.
settings.title: Settings
settings.locale.title: Language & Region
settings.locale.description: Set your country, language, and currency for localized content
settings.translation.title: Content Translation
settings.translation.apiKeyHelp: Stored locally. Never sent to 4DA servers.
settings.translation.embeddingMultilingu: For multilingual content, use {{model}} in AI Provider settings
settings.monitoring.title: Background Updates
settings.monitoring.closeToTrayDescripti: Hide window to system tray instead of quitting
settings.monitoring.backgroundRefreshDes: Run a background system task that keeps your feed fresh on your monitoring interval, even when 4DA isn't open.
settings.monitoring.backgroundRefreshAct: On — a system task refreshes your feed about every {{minutes}} min, even when 4DA is closed.
settings.monitoring.backgroundRefreshUns: Not available on this platform yet.
settings.monitoring.backgroundRefreshFai: Could not update the system task. Check your permissions and try again.
settings.monitoring.backgroundRefreshWha: Adds a Windows scheduled task ("4DA Background Refresh") that runs 4DA's fetch and scoring about every {{minutes}} minutes — the same work it does while open. Everything stays on your machine; nothing is sent anywhere. It runs only while you're signed in, needs no admin rights, and is removed when you turn this off.
settings.monitoring.backgroundRefreshNud: Keep your feed fresh when 4DA is closed
4DA only gathers signals while it's open. Turn on background refresh and it keeps your feed current even when the app is closed.
Background refresh is on — your feed stays fresh even when 4DA is closed.
settings.ai.title: AI Provider
settings.ai.baseUrlRequired: Base URL is required for this provider
settings.ai.rerankNote: Improves precision but uses API tokens
settings.context.title: Automatic Context Discovery
settings.context.description: Scan directories that define who you are - projects, notes, documents
settings.dataHealth.title: Data Health
settings.dataHealth.maintenanceDesc: Removes expired data that has already been processed and is no longer needed. Think of it as emptying the recycling bin — your intelligence stays, the clutter goes.
settings.dataHealth.whenToUse: Recommended monthly, or when the database size indicator above shows "Growing" or "Needs attention".
settings.dataHealth.alreadyClean: Database is already clean — nothing to remove.
settings.digest.title: Weekly Summary
settings.digest.description: Save filtered results as a digest file after each analysis
settings.personalization.title: Manual Adjustments
settings.personalization.description: Fine-tune your context with interests and exclusions
settings.personalization.suggestedDescri: Based on your detected tech stack and activity
settings.license.title: License
settings.license.expiredOn: Your license expired on {{date}}. Renew to restore Signal features.
settings.license.expiredGeneric: Your license expired. Renew to restore Signal features.
settings.license.recovery.title: Lost your license key?
settings.license.recovery.explanation: If you purchased a Signal license and can no longer find your key (e.g., after reinstalling or if your system reset), you can retrieve it here.
settings.license.recovery.emailHint: Enter the email address you used when purchasing your license. This is the email associated with your payment.
settings.license.recovery.privacy: Your email is sent only to 4da.ai to look up your license. No other data leaves your machine.
settings.license.recovery.notFound: No license found for this email. Check you're using the same email from your purchase. If you received your key directly, enter it in the activation field above.
settings.license.recovery.expired: Your license has expired. Renew at 4da.ai/signal to get a new key.
settings.license.recovery.networkError: Could not reach the recovery server. Check your internet connection and try again.
settings.license.recovery.rateLimited: Too many attempts. Please wait a moment before trying again.
settings.ai.intelligenceEngineDesc: Built-in models that power scoring and relevance — runs locally, no API key required.
settings.ai.autoAssessDesc: When a dependency newly surfaces as a blind spot, run the AI triage automatically so the worth-reviewing list stays current. Cached by your dependency set — it only uses your model when something actually changes.
settings.ai.byokNudgeTitle: Get better results with a cloud API key
settings.ai.byokNudgeBody: Local models work offline but produce lower quality analysis. Cloud providers (Anthropic, OpenAI) give significantly more accurate results. Your key goes direct to the provider — 4DA never stores or proxies it. Typical cost: under $1/month.
settings.ai.briefNarrated: AI-narrated — your model is strong enough for full brief synthesis.
settings.ai.briefFloorWeak: Served as 4DA's deterministic brief — grounded, private, and works offline. For an AI-narrated brief, choose a Sonnet-class model (Claude Sonnet or Opus, GPT-4o, or a 70B+ local model).
settings.ai.briefFloorNoLlm: Served as 4DA's deterministic brief — grounded, private, and works offline. Add a Sonnet-class model above to enable AI narration.
settings.ai.localOnlyNote: Embedding only — no LLM analysis. Add an API key for intelligence features.
settings.ai.keySavedSecure: API key saved in secure storage. Leave blank to keep it.
settings.ai.privacyNote: Your API key connects directly to the provider. 4DA never stores or proxies your key remotely.
panel.aiProviderHelp: Check that an API key is configured in Settings > General > AI Provider.
settings.attention.title: Attention Dashboard
settings.attention.description: Where your focus goes vs. where your code needs it
settings.attention.noData: Not enough interaction data yet. Use 4DA for a few days to build attention patterns.
settings.health.title: Project Health Radar
settings.health.noProjects: No projects detected. Add context directories in settings to enable health tracking.
settings.display.showAllViewsDescription: Display all navigation tabs regardless of usage
settings.digest.privacyNote: Sent directly from your machine via your email provider. 4DA never sees your email address or digest contents.

preemption.otherTargets.badgeHint: Affects a build target you don't build on this machine — surfaced but de-prioritised.
preemption.empty.title: Your stack is clean
preemption.empty.subtitle: No verified threats detected. Intelligence is actively monitoring.
preemption.locked.title: Stay ahead of dependency risk
preemption.locked.subtitle: Forward-looking alerts for dependency and ecosystem threats are a Signal feature. Start your free trial to unlock them.
preemption.freeFloor.title: OSV-verified security alerts are free for everyone

results.title: Analysis Results
results.clickAnalyze: Click Analyze to discover relevant content
results.startAnalysis: Run analysis to discover relevant content
results.howItWorks: 4DA scans sources like Hacker News, GitHub, Reddit, and arXiv, scores each item against your interests and tech stack, and surfaces only what matters to you.
results.analyzeHint: Your first analysis takes 1-2 minutes. After that, background monitoring keeps you updated automatically.
results.noMatch: No items match the current view
results.analyzedNoMatch: {{count}} items were analyzed, but none match the current filters.
results.nearMissesHint: These items scored close to your relevance threshold. Adjust interests in Settings to include them.
briefing.title: AI Briefing
briefing.staleNotice: New items found since this briefing.
briefing.profile.title: Your Profile
briefing.profile.interactHint: Interact with results to build affinities
briefing.profile.noDataYet: Analysis data will appear after first cycle

pulse.noData: No intelligence gathered yet. Run an analysis to get started.

pulse.newSinceLastSession_other: {{newItems}} new items since last session. {{newRelevant}} newly relevant.
pulse.noNewItems: No new items since your last session.
pulse.itemsAnalyzed: {{relevant}} of {{count}} items look relevant to your stack.
search.title: Search Your Intelligence
search.subtitle: Find signals, articles, and insights across your feed
search.tryDifferent: Try different keywords or a broader query
search.noAnalysisTitle: Intelligence Console ready — awaiting first analysis
search.noAnalysisHint: Run your first analysis to unlock the Intelligence Console
search.ungrounded: Ungrounded — no sources cited. Verify independently.
search.watchHint: Watch a search to get notified of new matches
update.defaultBody: A new version is ready to install.
browser.title: Desktop App Required
browser.description: 4DA runs as a desktop app to access your local files, monitor sources, and keep your data on your machine.
browser.hint: Run npm run tauri dev or launch the installed app.
onboarding.welcome.title: Welcome to 4DA
onboarding.welcome.description: 4DA reads the internet for developers — privately, locally — and gets sharper every day. It learns from how you engage with what it shows you, so yesterday's noise becomes tomorrow's signal.
onboarding.welcome.privateDesc: Runs on your machine. A local model keeps everything on-device; with your own cloud key, only what you analyze is sent — never through us.
onboarding.welcome.byokDesc: Your API keys, you control costs
onboarding.welcome.quickSetupHint: Quick setup — most steps are automatic
onboarding.welcome.skipHint: You can configure later in Settings
onboarding.choice.title: You're ready to go
onboarding.choice.description: 4DA is already learning about your projects in the background. You can start exploring now or fine-tune your setup first.
onboarding.choice.scanProjectsDesc: 100% local — nothing ever leaves your machine. Personalizes 4DA to your real stack.
onboarding.choice.scanning: Scanning your projects… this stays on your device
onboarding.choice.keywordOnlyHint: Semantic search still works out of the box. Scan your projects or add a provider anytime in Settings.
onboarding.trialHint: All Signal features are available free for 14 days. Start your trial anytime in Settings.
onboarding.apiKeys.title: API Keys
onboarding.apiKeys.subtitle: Configure API keys for semantic search, or start with keyword matching.
onboarding.apiKeys.pullWaitMessage: This may take a few minutes depending on your connection.
onboarding.apiKeys.ollamaReady: Ollama ready — no API keys needed.
onboarding.apiKeys.ollamaNoModels: Ollama detected but no models installed. Models will be downloaded automatically.
onboarding.apiKeys.openaiDesc: Used for semantic understanding (embeddings). Provides the best relevance scoring.
onboarding.apiKeys.openaiCostHint: ~$0.02/1M tokens for embeddings. Typical usage costs pennies/month.
onboarding.apiKeys.llmProviderDesc: Choose which AI to use for analyzing and explaining relevance.
onboarding.apiKeys.openaiSharedKeyHint: Using same OpenAI key for both embeddings and analysis.
onboarding.apiKeys.xApiDesc: Enables monitoring tweets from tech influencers. Requires a free X Developer account.
onboarding.apiKeys.xApiSkipHint: Skip this if you don't have an X developer account. You can add it later in Settings.
onboarding.apiKeys.keywordModeDesc: 4DA scores with keyword matching and ACE context synthesis — your projects, dependencies, and topics feed directly into scoring. Add an API key or Ollama any time in Settings for semantic search.
onboarding.complete.title: You're All Set!
onboarding.complete.subtitle: 4DA found relevant content for you. Here's how to make it even better.
onboarding.complete.settingsHint: Press Settings anytime to adjust your preferences
onboarding.interests.title: Your Interests
onboarding.interests.subtitle: Help 4DA understand what to surface. Your feedback refines scoring over time.
onboarding.interests.rolePlaceholder: e.g., Software Engineer, Product Manager, Researcher
onboarding.interests.placeholder: Type a topic and press Enter...
onboarding.interests.hint: Don't worry about being complete - 4DA uses your feedback and project context to improve scoring.
onboarding.context.title: Discover Your Context
onboarding.context.subtitle: 4DA discovers what matters to you by scanning your projects. 100% local, 100% private.
onboarding.context.continuousLearning: 4DA continuously monitors these directories for context.
onboarding.context.manageInSettings: You can manage directories anytime in Settings
onboarding.context.skipHint: Or skip this and add directories manually later
onboarding.context.faqFilesDesc: 4DA looks for project markers (package.json, Cargo.toml, README files, etc.), code files, and documents. It reads file names and contents to understand your work context.
onboarding.context.faqDataDesc: All scanning happens 100% locally on your machine. Your file contents never leave your computer. Only when you use the AI features, small text snippets are sent to your chosen AI provider (and you control that).
onboarding.context.faqUsageTitle: What does 4DA do with this information?
onboarding.context.faqUsageDesc: It builds a local understanding of your interests (e.g., "you work with Rust and React") to filter internet content and show you only what's relevant. This context stays in a local database on your machine.
onboarding.context.faqControlTitle: Can I control what gets scanned?
onboarding.context.faqControlDesc: Yes! You can add or remove directories anytime in Settings. 4DA automatically ignores sensitive locations like node_modules, .git folders, and hidden system files.
onboarding.context.faqTimeDesc: Usually a few seconds to a minute depending on how many projects you have. The initial scan is the longest - after that, 4DA only checks for changes.
onboarding.scan.title: Deep Intelligence Scan
onboarding.scan.subtitle: Comprehensive scan of 300-500+ items across all sources to build your personalized feed.
onboarding.scan.timeEstimate: This comprehensive scan may take 2-5 minutes
onboarding.scan.noRelevantItems: No highly relevant items found yet.
onboarding.scan.learnFromFeedback: 4DA will learn your preferences as you give feedback.
onboarding.scan.readyDesc: We'll comprehensively scan 300-500+ items from HN (5 categories), arXiv (16 fields), Reddit (40+ subs), and GitHub to build your personalized intelligence feed.
onboarding.results.title: Your Results
onboarding.results.topPicksSubtitle: Here are your top picks from across the internet.
onboarding.results.scanningMessage: Deep scanning HN, arXiv, Reddit, GitHub, RSS, YouTube...
onboarding.results.analyzingMessage: Analyzing hundreds of items for relevance...
onboarding.results.noRelevantItems: No highly relevant items found yet.
onboarding.results.learnFromFeedback: 4DA will learn your preferences as you give feedback.
onboarding.results.errorRetryHint: You can try again from the main app.
onboarding.results.backgroundScanHint: Full scan continues in background - you can enter anytime.
onboarding.results.backgroundScanDone: Full scan continues in background for comprehensive results.
onboarding.setup.title: Quick Setup
onboarding.setup.subtitle: Configure your AI and interests. Your data stays in a local database — you choose whether any cloud model sees it.
onboarding.setup.basicModeAvailable: Optional - keyword matching works without AI
onboarding.setup.skipDownload: Skip download — use keyword matching for now
onboarding.setup.allSectionsOptional: All sections optional — configure anytime in Settings
onboarding.setup.skipModelDownloadLabel: Continue without AI models (keyword search only)
onboarding.setup.keyFormatHintSoft: Key format may not be correct - you can still continue
onboarding.setup.usingTechStack: Using tech stack for content scoring
onboarding.setup.helpsScoring: Helps tailor content to your level
onboarding.experience.description: How do you engage with technology? This helps 4DA surface content at the right depth.
onboarding.experience.learning.label: Learning
onboarding.experience.learning.descripti: I'm learning new technologies and want tutorials
onboarding.experience.building.label: Building
onboarding.experience.building.descripti: I'm actively building projects and want practical content
onboarding.experience.leading.label: Leading
onboarding.experience.leading.descriptio: I lead technical decisions and want strategic insights
onboarding.experience.architecting.label: Architecting
onboarding.experience.architecting.descr: I design systems and want deep technical analysis
onboarding.setupAi.localAiReady: Local AI ready — no API keys needed
onboarding.setupAi.modelsNeeded: Ollama is running but its models aren't installed yet — local AI needs them first.
onboarding.setupAi.otherProviderHint: Enter your API key — configure base URL and model in Settings after setup.
onboarding.setupAi.configureInSettings: Base URL and model name can be configured in Settings → AI Provider.
onboarding.setupAi.envKeysDetected: API keys detected in your environment
onboarding.setupAi.orChooseCloud: for free local AI, or choose a cloud provider.
onboarding.setupAi.keywordHint: Private semantic search works out of the box — no key needed. An AI provider adds AI-written briefings and deeper reranking.
onboarding.setupAi.builtInSemanticTitle: Private semantic search is built in
onboarding.setupAi.builtInSemanticBody: It runs entirely on your device — nothing is downloaded or sent. An AI provider is optional and only adds AI-written briefings and deeper reranking.
onboarding.setupAi.basicModeHint: No worries — 4DA runs private on-device semantic search with no AI provider at all. Add Ollama or an API key later for AI briefings and deeper reranking.
onboarding.setupAi.byokExplainer: Your key, your account, direct to the provider. 4DA never stores or proxies your key. Typical cost: under $1/month.
onboarding.setupAi.localTradeoff: Fully offline. Runs on your CPU — a GPU just makes it faster. An 8B model is comfortable on 16 GB RAM; 14B+ gives the best quality.
onboarding.setupAi.privacyMessage: With a local model, your data never leaves your machine — no cloud account, no tracking. Add your own cloud key only if you want higher-accuracy analysis.
onboarding.setupAi.ollamaExplainer: Ollama runs AI models directly on your computer — completely private, completely free.
onboarding.setupAi.noKeyHint: No API key? No problem. 4DA runs private on-device semantic search by default. Add a key anytime for AI briefings and deeper reranking.
onboarding.calib.title: Your feed is calibrated
onboarding.calib.makeItYours: Remove or add — make it yours
onboarding.calib.interestsHint: These came from your responses. Correct anything that doesn't fit.
onboarding.calib.noInterests: No interests yet — add a few below.
settings.translations.title: Translation Editor
settings.translations.description: Override any translated string in the app
error.viewRecovery: An unexpected error occurred. Try again or switch views.
commandDeck.title: Command Deck
commandRunner.hint: Type a command and press Enter
pro.licenseExpired: Your license has expired. Renew to restore access.
pro.trialEnded: Your free trial has ended. Upgrade to continue.
trialExpiry.title_other: Your Signal trial ends in {{count}} days
trialExpiry.body: Preemption keeps its OSV security floor on Free. AI-assessed alerts, Blind Spots and more continue with Signal.
calibrationView.title: Calibration
calibrationView.nudge.title: Teach 4DA your taste in 5 minutes
calibrationView.nudge.body: A short calibration sharpens scoring and unlocks confidence tracking. Your responses never leave this machine.
calibrationView.settingsRow.title: Calibration
calibrationView.settingsRow.description: Take the taste test and label real items so scoring and confidence reflect your judgment.
calibrationView.settingsRow.progress_one: {{count}} of {{target}} labels — first calibration fit unlocks at {{target}}
calibrationView.settingsRow.progress_oth: {{count}} of {{target}} labels — first calibration fit unlocks at {{target}}
calibrationView.settingsRow.curveReady: Confidence curve fitted — calibration is active
calibrationView.sprint.labelProgress_one: {{count}} of {{target}} labels — first calibration fit unlocks at {{target}}
calibrationView.sprint.labelProgress_oth: {{count}} of {{target}} labels — first calibration fit unlocks at {{target}}
calibrationView.sprint.keyboardHint: Keyboard: → relevant · ← not relevant · Space skip
calibrationView.sprint.noItemsHint: Run an analysis first — review cards come from your scored corpus.
calibrationView.sprint.curveFitted: Confidence curve fitted — calibration is active
calibrationView.sprint.unlocks: Your labels are ground truth for confidence calibration and precision tracking.

documents.addFiles: Add PDF, DOCX, or other files to your scan directories to index them
community.title: Community Intelligence
community.description: Contributing anonymized scoring patterns to improve accuracy for all 4DA users.
settings.team.title: Team Sync
settings.team.adminDesc: Admins can invite members, manage settings, and configure sharing.
settings.team.memberDesc: Members can view shared signals, participate in decisions, and share their DNA.
settings.team.inviteExpiry: Invite codes expire after 72 hours and can only be used once.
settings.team.inviteReady: Share this code with your team member:
settings.team.inviteInstructions: The recipient should go to Settings > Team Sync > Join Team and enter this code.
settings.community.title: Community Intelligence
settings.community.subtitle: Help improve scoring for all 4DA users
team.dashboard.title: Team
team.decisions.title: Team Decisions
team.decisions.empty: No team decisions yet. Propose decisions from the Decision Log.
team.members.empty: No team members yet. Invite someone to get started.
team.notifications.title: Notifications
team.signals.empty: No shared signals yet. Team signals appear here when members share discoveries.
team.signals.hint: Share signals from your Signal Chains view, or enable auto-sharing in Team Settings.
team.sources.title: Shared Sources
team.sources.empty: No shared sources yet. Recommend sources your team should follow.
team.intelligence.title: Team Intelligence
team.intelligence.stack.title: Collective Tech Stack
team.intelligence.stack.empty: No collective stack data yet. Members need to share their Developer DNA first.
team.intelligence.blindSpots.title: Knowledge Blind Spots
team.intelligence.blindSpots.empty: No blind spots detected. Your team has strong coverage.
team.intelligence.overlap.title: Expertise Overlap Zones
team.intelligence.overlap.empty: No overlap zones detected yet. More member data needed.
team.intelligence.risk.title: Bus Factor Risk
team.intelligence.risk.empty: No single-expert risks detected. Good redundancy.
team.intelligence.empty: No team intelligence data yet. Members need to share their Developer DNA first.
enterprise.audit.title: Audit Log
enterprise.audit.description: Track organization-wide activities. All changes are immutable and timestamped.
enterprise.health.title: System Health
enterprise.webhooks.title: Webhooks
enterprise.webhooks.emptyDesc: Add a webhook to receive real-time notifications for team events.
enterprise.webhooks.name: Name
enterprise.webhooks.invalidUrl: URL must start with https:// or http://
enterprise.webhooks.httpsRecommended: HTTPS is recommended for production webhooks
enterprise.webhooks.circuitBroken: This webhook has been automatically disabled after 10 consecutive failures. Fix the endpoint and re-enable.
enterprise.docs.title: Webhook & API Reference
enterprise.docs.description: Technical reference for integrating 4DA webhooks with your infrastructure.
enterprise.export.title: Data Export & Portability
enterprise.export.description: Export your data for backup, compliance, or migration. No API keys are ever included.
enterprise.export.privacy: Exports never include API keys, passwords, tokens, or secrets. Source configurations are included without authentication credentials. All exports are stored locally in your data directory.
enterprise.diagnostics.title: Configuration Diagnostics
enterprise.diagnostics.description: Validate your setup and troubleshoot issues without contacting support.
enterprise.diagnostics.empty: Click "Run Diagnostics" to validate your configuration.
enterprise.diagnostics.licenseFree: Free tier — team features require Signal+ tier
enterprise.diagnostics.llmWarn: LLM not configured or unreachable — offline mode active
enterprise.diagnostics.ollamaWarn: Ollama not detected — using zero-vector fallback
enterprise.policies.title: Data Retention Policies
enterprise.policies.description: Control how long different data types are retained. A 7-day grace period applies before deletion.
enterprise.policies.info: Retention enforcement runs daily at 03:00 local time. Items within the 7-day grace period are flagged as "expiring soon" but not yet deleted. All cleanup operations are logged in the audit trail.
enterprise.policies.noRawContent: No raw content is ever shared. Only metadata, scores, and embeddings.
enterprise.policies.e2e: All team relay data is end-to-end encrypted (XChaCha20Poly1305).
enterprise.policies.localFirst: Primary data storage remains local to each machine.
enterprise.policies.relayDumb: The relay server cannot read encrypted payloads. "Dumb relay, smart clients."
enterprise.org.title: Organization
enterprise.org.notConfigured: No organization configured. Organization management is available on the Enterprise tier.
enterprise.sso.title: Single Sign-On (SSO)
enterprise.sso.description: Configure SAML or OIDC authentication for your organization.
enterprise.sso.notConfigured: SSO is not configured. Set up SAML or OpenID Connect to authenticate team members.
enterprise.sso.info: SSO verification happens locally on your machine. 4DA never sends credentials to any server. Your identity provider authenticates directly to the desktop app via a localhost callback.
enterprise.wizard.title: Team Setup Wizard
enterprise.wizard.description: Set up your team in 4 steps. All communication is end-to-end encrypted.
enterprise.wizard.teamConfigured: Your team is already configured. Manage members and settings in the Team tab.
enterprise.wizard.relayNote: Use the default managed relay or enter your self-hosted relay URL.
enterprise.wizard.inviteNote: Share this code securely with your team member. It expires in 24 hours.
enterprise.wizard.configureInfo: Your team is ready. Here are recommended next steps:
enterprise.wizard.doneNote: Your encrypted team relay is active. Members can join using invite codes.
enterprise.wizard.security: All team communication uses XChaCha20Poly1305 encryption. The relay server cannot read your data — it only stores and routes encrypted blobs. "Dumb relay, smart clients."
feedback.topicSuppressed: Suppressing '{{topic}}' — matching content will be filtered out.
feedback.watchAdded: Watching '{{topic}}' — you'll see new signals for this topic.
results.byokHint: Add an API key in Settings for more accurate analysis.
ollama.hintFreeLocal: Ollama provides free, local AI processing — no API key or account needed.

firstRun.scanningDescription: 20 sources including Hacker News, GitHub, Reddit, arXiv, CVE databases, and package registries — scored against your interests and tech stack.
firstRun.keywordHint: Using keyword matching. Add an AI provider in Settings for deeper semantic results.
firstRun.keywordMatching: Matching via keywords, dependencies, and context synthesis
firstRun.errorEmbedding: Semantic embeddings unavailable — scoring via keyword matching with context synthesis.
firstRun.errorFetch: Could not reach content sources. Check your internet connection.
firstRun.basicModeExplainer: 4DA scores with keyword matching and ACE context synthesis — your projects, dependencies, and topics feed directly into relevance. Add Ollama or an API key in Settings any time for semantic search.
firstRun.basicModeHint: Add Ollama or an API key for semantic search
firstRun.settingsHint: You can configure AI providers anytime in Settings
firstRun.coversGap: Covers a technology gap in your profile
firstRun.dnaHint: We're now watching for relevant content about your stack
shortcuts.title: Keyboard Shortcuts
about.verifiableDescription: Development process is auditable via the public git history. Every commit, co-authorship tag, and architectural decision is preserved in the repository.
about.attributionCreatorBefore: 4DA was conceived, designed, and directed by
about.attributionCreatorAfter: (4DA Systems) — product vision, architecture decisions, design philosophy, quality standards, and every choice that defines what 4DA is.
about.attributionClaudeBefore: The codebase was primarily engineered through collaborative sessions with
about.attributionClaudeAfter: (Anthropic — Opus 4.5 and Opus 4.6 models), serving as the principal implementation engine across thousands of iterative development cycles.
about.attributionLegitimacy: This human-directed, AI-implemented development model is a legitimate and increasingly standard approach to software engineering. The complete git history serves as a verifiable, externally auditable record of this process.
about.tagline: All rights reserved. Built with vision, engineered with Claude.
about.frameworkSubtitle: A Philosophy for Private Developer Intelligence
about.frameworkDesc: PASIFA scoring, Authority Stack governance, and the Autonomous Operations System — the philosophy behind 4DA, published openly.
about.geometrySubtitle: 4DA’s core invariants map to Platonic geometry. Each claim below is verifiable against the referenced source files.
chapters.title: Source Chapters
chapters.subtitle: Browse content by category — each chapter groups related sources together
chapters.empty: No items from these sources yet. They will appear after the next analysis cycle.
chapters.cat.securityDesc: Vulnerabilities, advisories, and patches affecting your stack

scoreDrawer.promptVersionTooltip: Prompt template version. Bumping invalidates prior calibration cohorts.

comp.nav.tradeoffsDesc: What we chose not to build
comp.approach.title: A Different Kind of Tool
comp.approach.desc: Most developer tools either aggregate content (you choose what to follow) or answer questions (you decide what to ask). 4DA does neither. It monitors the landscape and surfaces what's relevant to your work — before you think to look for it.
comp.approach.proactiveBody: You don't search 4DA. You don't curate feeds. 4DA scans your codebase, learns your technology stack, and continuously filters thousands of signals down to the few that matter to your specific work.
comp.approach.intelBody: A feed reader shows you everything in your subscriptions. A search engine answers what you ask. 4DA filters 92% of content through a multi-axis scoring pipeline, showing you only what crosses a confidence threshold calibrated to your context. Your actual rejection rate is computed from your data and shown in the Evidence tab.
comp.approach.transparentBody: Every score is fully explainable. You can see exactly why a piece of content was surfaced — which axes contributed, what context matched, how the confirmation gate evaluated it. Nothing is hidden behind "the algorithm."
comp.approach.categoryBody: People often ask "is 4DA like a feed reader?" or "is it like an AI search tool?" The honest answer is no — not because those are bad tools, but because the interaction model is fundamentally different. Feed readers require curation. Search requires intent. 4DA requires neither. It works in the background, learning from your projects, and interrupts you only when something genuinely matters.
comp.privacy.title: Privacy by Architecture
comp.privacy.desc: 4DA's privacy isn't a policy — it's a structural property of how the software is built. Local-first and direct-to-provider. There is no 4DA-operated server, so your indexed content, scores, and decisions have nowhere to go except your own SQLite database. The only outbound traffic is source-adapter fetches of public content, BYOK LLM calls to providers you chose, updater checks, license validation, and opt-in crash reports — every connection documented with source-code references.
comp.privacy.embeddingsValue: Local Ollama or your own API key
comp.privacy.embeddingsDetail: BYOK — we never see the key
comp.privacy.devBody: Your codebase is your most sensitive asset. Any tool that scans it to improve relevance needs to be trustworthy at the architectural level, not just the policy level. 4DA is a native desktop app — the code runs on your machine; your indexed data stays on your machine, and a cloud model sees only what you send it.
comp.privacy.byokBody: Bring Your Own Key means you have a direct billing relationship with your LLM provider. 4DA never touches, stores, or proxies your API keys beyond your OS keychain. You can verify this in the source code — the license makes it available.
comp.scoring.title: PASIFA Scoring Pipeline
comp.scoring.desc: Every piece of content goes through an 8-phase pipeline before you see it. The result is a score you can inspect, understand, and trust.
comp.scoring.p3Value: Check 5-axis confirmation (context, interest, ACE, learned, dependency)
comp.scoring.p4Value: KNN vector similarity against your project embeddings
comp.scoring.p6Value: Engagement signals, learned preferences, dependency matches
comp.scoring.p7Value: Apply confirmation gate — single-signal items capped below threshold
comp.scoring.p8Value: Composite score with full breakdown available
comp.scoring.gateBody: A single signal can never push content past the relevance threshold. At least 2 of the 5 axes must independently confirm relevance. This eliminates noise from clickbait titles that match a keyword but lack genuine relevance.
comp.scoring.explainBody: Click any item's score to see exactly which axes contributed, what context matched, and why it was surfaced. No black boxes — every number traces back to a specific input.
comp.context.title: Automatic Context Discovery
comp.context.desc: 4DA doesn't ask you to describe your interests. It discovers them by scanning your local projects — package files, imports, framework configurations, documentation.
comp.context.zeroBody: Point 4DA at your project directories. That's it. Within seconds, it builds a context profile that informs every relevance decision. No keyword lists, no topic selection, no manual curation.
comp.context.evolveBody: As your projects change, your context changes. Add a new dependency, start using a new framework — 4DA picks it up on the next scan. Your relevance profile stays current without manual intervention.
comp.compound.title: Compound Intelligence
comp.compound.desc: Every interaction teaches 4DA something about what matters to you. Over time, the system builds a behavioural model that makes scoring increasingly precise — unique to you, stored only on your machine.
comp.compound.behaviourBody: When you read, save, dismiss, or ignore content, 4DA records these signals. Items you engage with inform future scoring. Items you consistently skip get deprioritised. This happens locally — no data leaves your machine.
comp.compound.perUserBody: Cloud services learn from millions of users to build an average profile. 4DA learns from one user — you. The model is specific to your interests, your projects, your patterns. Two developers using 4DA on different codebases will see entirely different results.
comp.compound.inspectBody: Your learned preferences aren't hidden. You can see what 4DA has learned, why certain topics are boosted or suppressed, and reset any learned behaviour you disagree with. The learning is a tool, not a trap.
comp.tradeoffs.title: Honest Trade-offs
comp.tradeoffs.desc: Every architectural choice has a cost. Here's what 4DA intentionally chose — and what you give up by choosing it.
comp.tradeoffs.desktopCost: No mobile app, no web version
comp.tradeoffs.localValue: Your data stays on your machine (a cloud model only sees what you send it, with your own key)
comp.tradeoffs.byokValue: You control costs and provider choice
comp.tradeoffs.byokCost: Requires an API key for full features
comp.tradeoffs.devCost: Not designed for general news consumption
comp.tradeoffs.precisionValue: 92% of content filtered — measured across 9 developer personas
comp.tradeoffs.explainCost: More complex UI than simple chronological feeds
comp.tradeoffs.notForYouTitle: When 4DA might not be for you
comp.tradeoffs.notForYou1: If you need mobile access, a polished mobile app from an established feed reader will serve you better.
comp.tradeoffs.notForYou2: If you want broad discovery across hundreds of topics, a general aggregator or community site gives you wider coverage.
comp.tradeoffs.notForYou3: If you prefer asking questions over ambient monitoring, an AI search tool fits that workflow better.
comp.tradeoffs.notForYou4: If you need team collaboration today, products with mature sharing features are further along.
comp.tradeoffs.forYou1: You're a developer who wants relevant technical content surfaced automatically from your codebase context.
comp.tradeoffs.forYou2: You care about privacy and want to verify it architecturally, not just trust a policy.
comp.tradeoffs.forYou3: You value precision — you'd rather see 5 highly relevant items than 500 unfiltered ones.
comp.tradeoffs.forYou4: You want a tool that compounds — getting smarter about your interests every day.
